stories.title: Japan backs US deep sea mining push in new pact
stories.description: The non-binding memorandum, signed after a Trump-Takaichi meeting, was circulated at a UN seabed authority session where 40 nations seek a mining moratorium.

stories.title: Iran war wipes $11.5 trillion from global markets in one month
stories.description: The conflict's closure of the Strait of Hormuz has sent oil prices surging roughly 45% and left investors with few safe havens.

stories.title: Japan's Kikai supervolcano is refilling with magma, study finds
stories.description: Researchers mapped a 220-cubic-kilometer magma reservoir beneath the caldera that produced the Holocene's largest eruption 7,300 years ago.

stories.title: IAEA says no radiation risk after strikes hit Iranian facilities
stories.description: The agency confirmed Khondab and Khuzestan facilities were struck but contained no risk, even as warnings over Bushehr intensified.

stories.title: House passes 60-day DHS stopgap, extending record shutdown
stories.description: The bill funds all of DHS through May 22, but Senate Democrats call it "dead on arrival" as senators leave for a two-week recess.

stories.title: Trump floats renaming Strait of Hormuz after himself
stories.description: At a Saudi-backed investor summit in Miami Beach, the president jokingly called the contested waterway the "Strait of Trump" — but aides say the idea isn't entirely a joke.

stories.title: Medvedev warns nuclear conflict between Israel and Iran is now 'inevitable'
stories.description: Russia's deputy Security Council chairman blamed U.S. and Israeli military action for accelerating nuclear proliferation risks across the Middle East.

stories.title: DC airports grounded again after odor forces evacuation of air traffic facility
stories.description: The Potomac TRACON in Virginia was evacuated Friday evening for the second time in two weeks, halting flights at five airports.

stories.title: Trump demands Iran reopen Strait of Hormuz for peace deal
stories.description: Speaking at a Miami investment summit, the president jokingly called the blockaded waterway the "Strait of Trump" as the U.S. awaits Iran's response to a 15-point peace proposal.

stories.title: Poland's Tusk warns of imminent escalation in Iran war
stories.description: The Polish prime minister cited allied intelligence suggesting the conflict will intensify, not stabilize, in the coming days.

stories.title: Iran fires cluster munitions at Tel Aviv, killing one
stories.description: A day of dramatic escalation saw Israel strike two Iranian nuclear facilities for the first time in the war, prompting retaliatory missile fire that killed a man in Tel Aviv.

stories.title: Trump unveils farm aid at White House amid tariff, war pressures
stories.description: The president announced new SBA loan guarantees and biofuel standards before more than 800 farmers as the sector struggles with rising costs.

stories.title: Judge threatens VA with contempt over union contract defiance
stories.description: The agency re-terminated a collective bargaining agreement covering 320,000 workers hours before a compliance hearing, the judge said.

stories.title: Rapper Balendra Shah sworn in as Nepal's youngest PM
stories.description: The 35-year-old former mayor of Kathmandu is also the first person from the Madhesi community to lead the Himalayan nation.

stories.title: Trump presses Saudi Arabia on Abraham Accords at Miami summit
stories.description: The president used his closing address at the FII PRIORITY summit to again urge Riyadh to normalize relations with Israel amid the ongoing Iran conflict.

stories.title: Iran agrees to let humanitarian aid pass through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The first breakthrough at the contested waterway in a month of war came as the UN launched a task force to protect global fertilizer trade.

stories.title: Hezbollah confirms more than 400 fighters killed as Israel conflict grows
stories.description: The first comprehensive casualty tally emerges as Israel claims an even higher toll of 700 in its expanding air and ground campaign in Lebanon.

stories.title: Judge presses DOJ over FBI seizure of Fulton County 2020 ballots
stories.description: A Trump-appointed federal judge heard arguments Friday as Fulton County fights to recover more than 630 boxes of election materials seized in January.

stories.title: China protests after scholar's suicide tied to US interrogation
stories.description: Beijing formally protested to Washington and demanded a full investigation after a Chinese postdoctoral researcher died by suicide following law enforcement questioning.

stories.title: IRGC claims destruction of US refueling fleet at Al-Kharj base
stories.description: Iran says its 84th wave of strikes hit aerial refueling aircraft and logistical assets at the key Saudi facility supporting U.S. operations.

stories.title: Iran death toll tops 1,900 as IFRC warns of deepening crisis
stories.description: The Red Cross federation said Friday that at least 20,000 have been injured since U.S.-Israeli strikes began on Feb. 28, with relief efforts strained.

stories.title: Trump takes aim at Cuba in closing address at Miami summit
stories.description: Havana has turned to Pope Leo XIV to mediate with Washington as a U.S. oil embargo pushes the island toward economic collapse.

stories.title: China and Pakistan agree to jointly push for ceasefire in Iran war
stories.description: Wang Yi and Ishaq Dar aligned on an immediate ceasefire as Pakistan relays indirect talks between Washington and Tehran over a 15-point U.S. proposal.

stories.title: Iran strikes Israel, Gulf states as IRGC warns civilians to flee US bases
stories.description: On the 28th day of the war, Iran launched missiles and drones at Israel, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, and the UAE as Israel vowed to escalate strikes on Tehran.

stories.title: Trump signals to allies no imminent Iran ground invasion
stories.description: The reassurance comes as the Pentagon weighs sending 10,000 more troops and Sen. Murkowski drafts a war authorization bill.

stories.title: Witkoff says he expects Iran talks 'this week' on 15-point peace plan
stories.description: The U.S. envoy said Friday that Washington is awaiting Tehran's response to its proposal, delivered through Pakistan as intermediary.

stories.title: Gas prices near $4 as states split on tax holidays
stories.description: The national average hit $3.98, up a dollar in one month, spurring federal and state gas tax suspension proposals with mixed results.

stories.title: Hegseth struck Black and female officers from Army promotion list
stories.description: The defense secretary overrode Army leaders' objections, removing two Black and two female officers from a one-star general list, according to the New York Times.

stories.title: Mexico says 40,000 of 130,000 missing may be alive
stories.description: President Sheinbaum presented a long-awaited report on Mexico's disappearance crisis, revealing a national database plagued by gaps, duplicates, and missing data.

stories.title: Iran-linked hackers breach FBI director's personal email
stories.description: The Handala Hack Team posted photos and documents from Kash Patel's Gmail account; the DOJ confirmed the materials appear authentic.

stories.title: Idaho passes strictest transgender bathroom ban with felony penalties
stories.description: The bill, which now heads to the governor's desk, criminalizes restroom use in both government buildings and private businesses.

stories.title: G7 agrees to multinational Hormuz escort mission after Iran war ends
stories.description: Foreign ministers meeting in France committed to restoring freedom of navigation in the strait, with over 30 nations signaling readiness to contribute.

stories.title: DOJ issues subpoenas in Paramount-Warner Bros merger probe
stories.description: Federal investigators are examining the $110 billion deal's impact on streaming competition, content rights, and movie theaters.

stories.title: RFK Jr. ally says White House now sees vaccines as 'losing issue'
stories.description: A federal court ruling, a key adviser's resignation, and growing Senate resistance are forcing the administration to retreat from Kennedy's vaccine overhaul.

stories.title: Rubio says US expects Iran war to end in weeks
stories.description: The secretary of state told G7 ministers that U.S. objectives can be met without ground troops, calling the campaign ahead of schedule.

stories.title: Controllers say LaGuardia needed more staff night of deadly crash
stories.description: Six air traffic controllers told Reuters the airport's workload on March 22 warranted additional staffing before the fatal collision.

stories.title: House ethics panel finds Rep. Cherfilus-McCormick guilty of 25 violations
stories.description: A bipartisan committee ruled the Florida Democrat funneled $5 million in COVID-19 relief funds into her 2022 campaign, setting up a possible expulsion vote.

stories.title: US land mines found near Shiraz kill civilians in first such deployment in decades
stories.description: Weapons experts identified American-made BLU-91/B anti-tank mines scattered near an Iranian missile base, drawing condemnation from human rights groups.

stories.title: Deployed nuclear warheads rose again in 2025, report finds
stories.description: The annual Nuclear Weapons Ban Monitor counted 4,012 warheads on missiles, submarines, and bomber bases, up 108 from 2024.

stories.title: Iran accuses US-Israel of genocide at UN Human Rights Council
stories.description: Foreign Minister Araghchi addressed an emergency session Friday, calling the Minab school airstrike the "tip of a far larger iceberg" of war crimes.

stories.title: US can confirm only a third of Iran's missiles destroyed
stories.description: Intelligence assessments contradict Trump's claim that Iran has "very few rockets left" as the war nears its one-month mark.

stories.title: SpaceX IPO sparks $15M in ticker bets before filing
stories.description: Polymarket wagers on SpaceX's ticker symbol, valuation, and exchange have topped $15.2 million even though the company has yet to file IPO paperwork.

stories.title: Buy America law stalls affordable housing amid HUD waiver backlog
stories.description: Developers say HUD is taking six months or more to process waivers, adding hundreds of thousands of dollars in costs to federally funded projects.

stories.title: IRGC urges civilians to flee areas near US forces as Trump extends Hormuz deadline
stories.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard declared the Strait of Hormuz closed and threatened US military positions as Trump pushed his strike deadline to April 6.

stories.title: Asia-Pacific governments rush to calm markets as Middle East war drives energy crisis
stories.description: From emergency budgets to fuel rationing plans, countries across the region are deploying fiscal packages and liquidity measures to counter the economic fallout.

stories.title: Iran halts two Chinese ships near Hormuz in rare reversal
stories.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard hardened its blockade Friday, intercepting vessels and declaring the strait closed to ships linked to U.S. allies.

stories.title: US confirms killing of IRGC Navy chief Tangsiri in Bandar Abbas strike
stories.description: CENTCOM verified the death of the architect of Iran's Strait of Hormuz blockade, calling on remaining IRGC Navy members to abandon their posts.

stories.title: Kremlin denies Putin asked oligarchs to fund Ukraine war costs
stories.description: Spokesman Peskov said a billionaire volunteered a large donation on his own, contradicting reports that Putin directly appealed for budget contributions.

stories.title: Study finds cells, firms and cities all slow in adding new functions as they grow
stories.description: Researchers identified a shared mathematical pattern governing how complex systems diversify, from bacteria to corporations to government agencies.

stories.title: US burns through 850-plus Tomahawks in Iran war, alarming Pentagon
stories.description: More than 20% of the estimated U.S. Tomahawk stockpile has been expended in four weeks of strikes, the Washington Post reported.

stories.title: Rubio calls Ukraine 'not America's war' as peace talks stall
stories.description: U.S. Secretary of State Rubio's pointed remark at the G7 came as Russia launched a record 948-drone barrage and no new peace talks are scheduled.

stories.title: ECB survey shows Iran war upended eurozone inflation outlook
stories.description: Consumer confidence across Europe hit its lowest since October 2023 as the Middle East conflict drives energy prices higher and revives inflation fears.

stories.title: Epstein survivors sue Trump admin and Google over exposed identities
stories.description: About 100 victims filed a class action alleging the DOJ's mass document release exposed their identities and Google refused to remove the information.

stories.title: Ukraine, Saudi Arabia sign defense deal during Zelensky's surprise visit
stories.description: Kyiv is trading its battle-tested drone warfare expertise for advanced weapons and investment as both nations face Iranian-made threats.

stories.title: China launches two trade barrier probes against U.S.
stories.description: Beijing's Commerce Ministry opened investigations into U.S. supply chain disruptions and green trade restrictions, retaliating against Washington's Section 301 probes.

stories.title: US has lost at least 16 aircraft in Iran war as losses mount
stories.description: The toll includes an F-35A damaged by Iranian ground fire on March 19 — the first time the stealth jet has been struck by enemy fire.

stories.title: Mediators say Iran never requested strike pause Trump cited
stories.description: President Trump extended the deadline for energy strikes to April 6, but mediators told the WSJ that Tehran made no such request.

stories.title: Anthropic discusses Q4 2026 IPO that could raise over $60B
stories.description: Bankers competing for the mandate expect the AI startup's listing to rank among the largest IPOs in history, according to The Information.

stories.title: Iran claims 1 million fighters ready for US ground invasion
stories.description: Tehran says volunteers are flooding Basij and IRGC centers as the U.S. sends 82nd Airborne paratroopers and Marines to the region.

stories.title: Experts warn Iran war's environmental toll could persist for decades
stories.description: Over 400 environmentally harmful incidents have been logged since the conflict began, with toxic pollution threatening air, water, and soil across the Gulf.

stories.title: UK pledges £100M more for Ukraine's air defenses
stories.description: The additional funding brings Britain's air defense contributions to roughly £600 million over two months as Russia's aerial attacks persist.

stories.title: Trump pushes back on report he wants speedy Iran deal
stories.description: At a Thursday cabinet meeting, the president called The Wall Street Journal "terrible" and said Iran, not the U.S., is the side seeking a resolution.

stories.title: Houthi leader signals readiness to join Iran war against US and Israel
stories.description: Ansarallah has refrained from entering the conflict since it began Feb. 28 but controls the strategic Bab al-Mandeb Strait.

stories.title: Iran offers Spain Hormuz access in first such EU overture
stories.description: Tehran cited Madrid's "commitment to international law" after Spain barred US forces from using its bases for strikes on Iran.

stories.title: Pentagon confirms first use of drone boats in Iran conflict
stories.description: Uncrewed speedboats built by BlackSea Technologies have been patrolling in support of Operation Epic Fury, the Pentagon said Thursday.

stories.title: Education Dept. to vacate headquarters for smaller office amid dismantling push
stories.description: The move, planned for August, comes as the agency's building sits 70% vacant after more than a year of mass layoffs under the Trump administration.

stories.title: Bipartisan Senate bill targets insider trading on prediction markets
stories.description: Four senators introduced legislation Thursday to ban government officials from using nonpublic information to trade on prediction market platforms.

stories.title: US senators introduce bill to sanction Hungary over Ukraine aid blockade
stories.description: Sens. Shaheen and Tillis unveiled the bipartisan Block Putin Act targeting Hungarian officials as Orban continues to hold up a €90 billion EU loan to Kyiv.

stories.title: Trump's signature to appear on US currency, ending 165-year tradition
stories.description: The Treasury Department said $100 bills with the president's name will begin printing in June, replacing the Treasurer's signature for the first time since 1861.

stories.title: Study finds AI chatbots affirm users 49% more than humans do
stories.description: Stanford researchers found all 11 major AI systems tested exhibited sycophancy, giving overly agreeable advice that made users less willing to fix relationships.

stories.title: Senate Democrats block photo ID voting amendment on party-line vote
stories.description: All 47 Senate Democrats voted against a standalone photo ID requirement for federal elections, handing Republicans a midterm messaging win.

stories.title: Deputy AG Blanche says DOJ purged all staff tied to Trump prosecutions
stories.description: Speaking at CPAC on Thursday, Blanche said more than 200 people have left and no FBI agents involved in Trump cases remain.

stories.title: US conducts hypersonic missile test at Cape Canaveral
stories.description: Witnesses spotted an unscheduled launch Thursday from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station, identified by observers as a hypersonic missile test during a pre-announced window.

stories.title: UK authorizes military to board Russian shadow fleet tankers
stories.description: Starmer announced the move at a Helsinki summit as a drone struck a sanctioned oil tanker laden with Russian crude near the Bosporus.

stories.title: US jobless claims rise to 210,000; continuing claims hit lowest since May 2024
stories.description: Continuing claims fell to 1.819 million, suggesting laid-off workers are finding new jobs quickly despite broader economic uncertainty.

stories.title: DOJ opens civil rights probes into three medical schools' admissions
stories.description: Stanford, Ohio State, and UC San Diego must turn over seven years of applicant data by April 24 or risk losing federal funding.

stories.title: Trump pushes aides to end Iran war within weeks
stories.description: The president privately told advisers the conflict is in its final stages, urging them to stick to a four-to-six-week timeline.

stories.title: OECD warns US inflation could hit 4.2% amid Iran war oil shock
stories.description: The organization sharply raised its forecast from 3%, citing surging energy prices from the Middle East conflict that have disrupted global oil supplies.

stories.title: Judge refuses to dismiss drug charges against Maduro
stories.description: The former Venezuelan president appeared in a Manhattan federal court Thursday as his defense sought to toss the case over a legal fees dispute.

stories.title: IBM says quantum computer matched lab data in materials simulation
stories.description: Researchers say quantum simulations of a magnetic crystal reproduced neutron scattering experiments, a feat many believed was beyond current hardware.

stories.title: Researchers recover 95% of battery metals using plasma and citric acid
stories.description: The patented method uses a 15-minute microwave plasma pretreatment and lemon-strength acid to extract valuable minerals from spent lithium-ion batteries.

stories.title: DOJ sues NewYork-Presbyterian over contracts that block cheaper care
stories.description: The Justice Department alleges the hospital system's deals with insurers prevented New Yorkers from accessing lower-cost health plans.

stories.title: Artemis 2 crew flies to Florida ahead of April 1 Moon launch
stories.description: The four astronauts are set to arrive at Kennedy Space Center on Friday as final pad preparations continue for the first crewed lunar mission since Apollo.

stories.title: Iran-backed FPV drones hit US helicopter, radar at Baghdad base
stories.description: Footage shows strikes on a Black Hawk and a NASAMS-linked radar at Victory Base, escalating cheap-drone threats to American forces in Iraq.

stories.title: Census: U.S. metro growth slowed sharply as immigration declined
stories.description: Border communities saw the steepest drops, with Laredo's growth rate falling from 3.2% to 0.2% as net international migration was cut in half.

stories.title: Iran demands Lebanon be included in any ceasefire deal
stories.description: Tehran told mediators it will not accept a deal with the U.S. and Israel that does not halt the Israeli military campaign against Hezbollah.

stories.title: LaGuardia wreckage towed off runway four days after fatal Air Canada crash
stories.description: Most of the roughly 40 hospitalized passengers have been released, while the NTSB investigates overlapping failures including a missing transponder on the fire truck.

stories.title: US kills four in 47th Caribbean boat strike since September
stories.description: Operation Southern Spear's death toll has reached roughly 163 as rights groups and victims' families challenge the legality of the campaign.

stories.title: France rules out military action to reopen Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Prime Minister Lecornu told the National Assembly that France "will not be drawn into a war that it did not choose" but will help secure shipping routes.

stories.title: Iran’s expanding long-range missiles pose growing threat
stories.description: Despite a 90% drop in launch rates, remaining Iranian missiles fired from hidden eastern bases are causing greater damage, according to analysts.

stories.title: Aid groups plead for funds as Middle East war strains humanitarian system
stories.description: UNHCR says operations are "dramatically underfunded" as millions are displaced and the dissolution of USAID leaves organizations scrambling to respond.

stories.title: White House misses deadline to nominate new CDC director
stories.description: The agency has lacked a permanent leader for over seven months as the administration struggles to find a candidate who can win Senate confirmation.

stories.title: GOP lawmakers blast Pentagon over Iran war plans after briefing
stories.description: House Armed Services Chair Mike Rogers warned of "consequences" after defense officials failed to explain troop deployments to lawmakers.

stories.title: DOJ admits ICE arrested immigrants at courts under wrong legal authority
stories.description: U.S. Attorney Jay Clayton said the government's defense of immigration courthouse arrests was based on a memo that only applied to criminal courthouses.

stories.title: Senate Republicans probe abortion pill makers, press FDA on online sales
stories.description: Sen. Cassidy and four GOP colleagues demanded compliance records from all three mifepristone manufacturers and urged the FDA to crack down on unauthorized sellers.

stories.title: Iran claims missile strike on USS Abraham Lincoln as Gulf war widens
stories.description: The IRGC launched attacks on Israel and U.S. bases in Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain as drones set fire to a fuel tank at Kuwait's airport.

stories.title: 8th Circuit backs Trump's no-bond immigration detention policy
stories.description: The 2-1 ruling is the second appellate victory for the administration's mass detention approach, despite opposition from hundreds of lower-court judges.

stories.title: US offers $3M bounty for financial information on Haitian gangs
stories.description: The reward targets financial networks of Viv Ansanm and Gran Grif, shifting tactics from previous bounties focused on individual gang leaders.

stories.title: Six Arab states demand Iraq halt militia attacks from its territory
stories.description: Baghdad summoned the U.S. envoy and vowed a UN complaint after a deadly airstrike on the Habbaniyah base killed seven soldiers.

stories.title: Surgeon general pick stalls as GOP senators balk
stories.description: Dr. Casey Means' nomination has languished for nearly 300 days, with key Republican senators still withholding support over vaccine views and qualifications.

stories.title: JetBlue shares surge on report it hired advisers to explore sale
stories.description: The struggling airline is reportedly evaluating potential deals with United Airlines, Alaska Airlines, or Southwest Airlines.

stories.title: Iranian analyst warns Tehran ready to seize UAE, Bahrain coastlines
stories.description: Morteza Simiari said on state TV that Iran's military has rehearsed coastal operations and will act if the U.S. "makes any mistake."

stories.title: Pew poll finds 61% of Americans disapprove of Trump's Iran war
stories.description: The survey of 3,524 adults, conducted March 16-22, also found 59% believe the U.S. made the wrong decision to use military force against Tehran.

stories.title: Senate GOP launches reconciliation bill for Iran war, ICE and voter ID
stories.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham said his Budget Committee will "expeditiously" draft a second party-line bill to bypass the filibuster, but key Republicans are skeptical.

stories.title: Think tank proposes $100K cap on Social Security benefits
stories.description: The Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget's "Six Figure Limit" plan would trim payouts to wealthy retirees as the program faces insolvency by 2032.

stories.title: White House rejects Musk's offer to pay TSA workers
stories.description: The rejection came as the DHS shutdown hit day 40, with a TSA official telling Congress that wait times have reached historic highs.

stories.title: Pentagon signs deals to quadruple missile output amid Iran war
stories.description: Lockheed Martin, BAE Systems, and Honeywell Aerospace agreed to accelerate production of key weapons as the U.S. replenishes wartime stockpiles.

stories.title: Fed's operating losses fell to $19B in 2025 from $78B
stories.description: The central bank's audited financial statements show interest expenses dropped sharply as rate cuts reduced what the Fed pays on bank reserves.

stories.title: Raskin says DOJ memo shows Trump showed classified map on plane
stories.description: A top House Democrat says the Trump administration's own Justice Department inadvertently handed over "damning evidence" from the classified documents probe.

stories.title: EPA waives summer smog rules on gasoline amid Iran war price surge
stories.description: The emergency waiver allows E15 ethanol-blend fuel sales starting May 1, as the Strait of Hormuz closure drives gas prices sharply higher.

stories.title: Meta, YouTube found negligent in social media addiction trial
stories.description: A Los Angeles jury awarded $3 million in damages to a young woman who alleged the platforms' addictive design features caused her mental health struggles.

stories.title: White House says congressional approval not needed for Iran strikes
stories.description: Spokeswoman Karoline Leavitt said the U.S. postponed strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure after "productive conversations" but warned Trump is "prepared to unleash hell."

stories.title: Indiana bars Piece by Piece Autism Centers from Medicaid
stories.description: The provider collected $29 million in 2023 to treat just 84 patients by exploiting a billing loophole that allowed reimbursements of up to $640 per hour.

stories.title: Supreme Court rules ISPs not liable for users' piracy
stories.description: The unanimous decision reverses a jury verdict against Cox Communications in a lawsuit led by Sony Music over illegal downloads of more than 10,000 songs.

stories.title: NASA climate scientist Kate Marvel quits, citing Trump's 'attacks on science'
stories.description: Marvel said a year of funding uncertainty and dismantled programs left her unable to do meaningful work at the Goddard Institute for Space Studies.

stories.title: California Democrats' own poll shows two Republicans leading governor's race
stories.description: The party warns its crowded field of eight candidates risks being shut out of the November general election under the state's top-two primary system.

stories.title: Astronomers spot two planets forming around a young star like our sun
stories.description: WISPIT 2 is only the second known system where two planets have been directly observed taking shape, offering a rare window into our solar system's origins.

stories.title: Trump sets May 14-15 for rescheduled Beijing summit with Xi
stories.description: The White House announced firm dates for the postponed China trip, which was delayed by the ongoing U.S. and Israeli war against Iran.

stories.title: New York Times accuses Pentagon of defying court order on press access
stories.description: The paper filed a new motion Tuesday after the department closed its press corridor and imposed escort requirements days after a judge struck down its media policy.

stories.title: Prosecutor admitted no crime evidence in DOJ probe of Fed's Powell
stories.description: A newly unsealed transcript shows the lead prosecutor told a judge his office had no evidence of criminal wrongdoing by the Fed chair.

stories.title: TSA chief tells Congress agency won't be ready for World Cup
stories.description: Nearly 460 officers have quit and unpaid payroll nears $1 billion as the DHS shutdown enters its 39th day, the acting TSA administrator testified.

stories.title: US Army raises max enlistment age to 42, eases marijuana rules
stories.description: Updated regulation published March 20 also drops the waiver requirement for a single marijuana possession conviction to speed up recruiting.

stories.title: Israeli airstrike hits Christian town north of Beirut for first time
stories.description: The strike on Sahel Alma marks a widening of Israel's air campaign into previously untouched areas of Lebanon amid the escalating war with Hezbollah.

stories.title: Trump gets daily strike montage on Iran war, raising concerns
stories.description: Officials told NBC News the curated two-minute videos emphasize U.S. successes while offering little detail on Iranian attacks or setbacks.

stories.title: US assessing how to resume nuclear weapons testing
stories.description: Undersecretary DiNanno told lawmakers the administration has made no decisions on a testing program but did not rule out underground explosive tests.

stories.title: DHS shutdown delayed NTSB investigators probing deadly LaGuardia crash
stories.description: NTSB Chair Jennifer Homendy said investigators waited hours in TSA lines, preventing the team from completing a full first day of work at the crash site.

stories.title: US ships 30,000 barrels of fuel to Cuba's private sector
stories.description: The Trump administration's dual strategy of blockading Cuba's government while supplying private businesses is gaining traction, according to Reuters.

stories.title: US airstrikes kill 15 Iraq PMF fighters, including Anbar commander
stories.description: The deadliest single strike since the US-Israeli war on Iran began also targeted PMF leader Falih al-Fayadh's residence in a separate attack.

stories.title: Netanyahu taps top aide Dermer to vet US-Iran deal
stories.description: Ron Dermer's mission focuses on ensuring Iran's nuclear program is dismantled and its enriched uranium stockpile eliminated, according to Bloomberg.

stories.title: Democrat flips Florida House seat in Trump's Mar-a-Lago district
stories.description: Emily Gregory defeated Trump-endorsed Republican Jon Maples in a district the president carried by about 10 points in 2024.

stories.title: Anduril, Palantir building Golden Dome missile shield software
stories.description: The Wall Street Journal reported the two firms are part of a nine-company consortium developing the $185 billion antimissile system's command-and-control layer.

stories.title: Denmark's left bloc wins election but falls short of majority
stories.description: Centrist leader Lars Løkke Rasmussen emerges as kingmaker with 14 seats as weeks of coalition talks loom.

stories.title: Senate blocks third Democratic bid to curb Trump's Iran war powers
stories.description: The 47-53 vote marked the third time this month Republicans defeated a resolution demanding congressional authorization for the military campaign.

stories.title: Death toll rises to 69 in Colombia military plane crash
stories.description: A C-130 Hercules went down shortly after takeoff Monday in the Amazon jungle, marking one of Colombia's deadliest military air disasters.

stories.title: Iran says drones struck Israeli defense sites, Ben Gurion Airport
stories.description: Tuesday's claimed strikes targeted Rafael Advanced Defense Systems and Israel Aerospace Industries as Operation True Promise 4 enters its fourth week.

stories.title: Judge orders Trump to unfreeze $3B in Chicago transit funds
stories.description: A federal judge granted a temporary restraining order releasing frozen rail funding, calling the administration's selective enforcement "pretextual."

stories.title: Macron urges Iran to negotiate in third call with Pezeshkian since war began
stories.description: The French president demanded an end to "unacceptable" attacks and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz as U.S. peace efforts intensify.

stories.title: Debris from intercepted missiles knocks out 7 Kuwait power lines
stories.description: Kuwait's Defense Ministry reported intercepting 17 ballistic missiles and 13 drones in 24 hours as the country endures its worst sustained bombardment since the Iran war began.

stories.title: Iran stops all gas exports to Turkey after South Pars strike
stories.description: The halt follows Israel's March 18 attack on the world's largest natural gas field, cutting off about 14% of Turkey's supply.

stories.title: Lebanon expels Iran's ambassador as Israel vows to occupy southern Lebanon
stories.description: Beirut declared Tehran's envoy persona non grata on Tuesday as Israel announced plans to seize territory up to the Litani River.

stories.title: British troops down 14 drones in record night at Iraq base
stories.description: RAF Regiment forces intercepted the largest single-night drone swarm since the Iran conflict began, as Tehran resumed attacks after an Eid lull.

stories.title: Israel says it killed Quds Force operative in Beirut strike
stories.description: The IDF and Shin Bet said a naval strike on Monday killed Muhammad Ali Kourani, who they described as an Iranian-directed operative planning attacks from Lebanon.

stories.title: Saudi crown prince urges Trump to press for Iran regime's fall
stories.description: Mohammed bin Salman has called the US-Israeli war a "historic chance" to reshape the Middle East as Gulf states edge toward open confrontation with Tehran.

stories.title: Rubio testifies at ex-roommate's secret Venezuela lobbying trial
stories.description: The secretary of state became the first sitting Cabinet member to take the stand in a criminal trial since 1983.

stories.title: Moldova declares 60-day energy emergency after Russian strikes sever key power line
stories.description: Parliament voted 72-0 to impose the emergency after overnight drone strikes on Ukraine knocked out the line supplying up to 70% of Moldova's electricity.

stories.title: Hezbollah's missile stockpile down to one-sixth of pre-war levels, Israel says
stories.description: Israel's military estimates the Iranian-backed group retains between 11,000 and 13,000 missiles and rockets, down from roughly 75,000 before October 2023.

stories.title: NASA outlines moon strategy as Artemis II targets April launch
stories.description: The agency is hosting a Washington event to detail how it plans to return astronauts to the lunar surface by 2028 despite ongoing delays.

stories.title: Aid flotilla docks in Havana as US oil blockade deepens Cuba's crisis
stories.description: The first vessel of the "Our America Convoy" delivered medical supplies, food, and solar panels, with two more ships expected this week.

stories.title: Brazil court places Bolsonaro under house arrest on health grounds
stories.description: Justice Alexandre de Moraes reversed months of denials, granting a 90-day house arrest after the ex-president was hospitalized with severe pneumonia.

stories.title: Iran war drives fertilizer costs higher as U.S. planting season nears
stories.description: Surging nitrogen prices, supplier shortages, and Strait of Hormuz disruptions are compounding years of losses for American grain farmers.

top.title: Japan backs US deep sea mining push in new pact
top.description: The non-binding memorandum, signed after a Trump-Takaichi meeting, was circulated at a UN seabed authority session where 40 nations seek a mining moratorium.

top.title: Iran war wipes $11.5 trillion from global markets in one month
top.description: The conflict's closure of the Strait of Hormuz has sent oil prices surging roughly 45% and left investors with few safe havens.

top.title: Japan's Kikai supervolcano is refilling with magma, study finds
top.description: Researchers mapped a 220-cubic-kilometer magma reservoir beneath the caldera that produced the Holocene's largest eruption 7,300 years ago.

top.title: IAEA says no radiation risk after strikes hit Iranian facilities
top.description: The agency confirmed Khondab and Khuzestan facilities were struck but contained no risk, even as warnings over Bushehr intensified.

top.title: House passes 60-day DHS stopgap, extending record shutdown
top.description: The bill funds all of DHS through May 22, but Senate Democrats call it "dead on arrival" as senators leave for a two-week recess.

top.title: Trump floats renaming Strait of Hormuz after himself
top.description: At a Saudi-backed investor summit in Miami Beach, the president jokingly called the contested waterway the "Strait of Trump" — but aides say the idea isn't entirely a joke.

top.title: Medvedev warns nuclear conflict between Israel and Iran is now 'inevitable'
top.description: Russia's deputy Security Council chairman blamed U.S. and Israeli military action for accelerating nuclear proliferation risks across the Middle East.

top.title: DC airports grounded again after odor forces evacuation of air traffic facility
top.description: The Potomac TRACON in Virginia was evacuated Friday evening for the second time in two weeks, halting flights at five airports.

top.title: Trump demands Iran reopen Strait of Hormuz for peace deal
top.description: Speaking at a Miami investment summit, the president jokingly called the blockaded waterway the "Strait of Trump" as the U.S. awaits Iran's response to a 15-point peace proposal.

top.title: Poland's Tusk warns of imminent escalation in Iran war
top.description: The Polish prime minister cited allied intelligence suggesting the conflict will intensify, not stabilize, in the coming days.

top.title: Iran fires cluster munitions at Tel Aviv, killing one
top.description: A day of dramatic escalation saw Israel strike two Iranian nuclear facilities for the first time in the war, prompting retaliatory missile fire that killed a man in Tel Aviv.

top.title: Trump unveils farm aid at White House amid tariff, war pressures
top.description: The president announced new SBA loan guarantees and biofuel standards before more than 800 farmers as the sector struggles with rising costs.

top.title: Judge threatens VA with contempt over union contract defiance
top.description: The agency re-terminated a collective bargaining agreement covering 320,000 workers hours before a compliance hearing, the judge said.

top.title: Rapper Balendra Shah sworn in as Nepal's youngest PM
top.description: The 35-year-old former mayor of Kathmandu is also the first person from the Madhesi community to lead the Himalayan nation.

top.title: Trump presses Saudi Arabia on Abraham Accords at Miami summit
top.description: The president used his closing address at the FII PRIORITY summit to again urge Riyadh to normalize relations with Israel amid the ongoing Iran conflict.

top.title: Iran agrees to let humanitarian aid pass through Strait of Hormuz
top.description: The first breakthrough at the contested waterway in a month of war came as the UN launched a task force to protect global fertilizer trade.

top.title: Hezbollah confirms more than 400 fighters killed as Israel conflict grows
top.description: The first comprehensive casualty tally emerges as Israel claims an even higher toll of 700 in its expanding air and ground campaign in Lebanon.

top.title: Judge presses DOJ over FBI seizure of Fulton County 2020 ballots
top.description: A Trump-appointed federal judge heard arguments Friday as Fulton County fights to recover more than 630 boxes of election materials seized in January.

top.title: China protests after scholar's suicide tied to US interrogation
top.description: Beijing formally protested to Washington and demanded a full investigation after a Chinese postdoctoral researcher died by suicide following law enforcement questioning.

top.title: IRGC claims destruction of US refueling fleet at Al-Kharj base
top.description: Iran says its 84th wave of strikes hit aerial refueling aircraft and logistical assets at the key Saudi facility supporting U.S. operations.

top.title: Iran death toll tops 1,900 as IFRC warns of deepening crisis
top.description: The Red Cross federation said Friday that at least 20,000 have been injured since U.S.-Israeli strikes began on Feb. 28, with relief efforts strained.

top.title: Trump takes aim at Cuba in closing address at Miami summit
top.description: Havana has turned to Pope Leo XIV to mediate with Washington as a U.S. oil embargo pushes the island toward economic collapse.

top.title: China and Pakistan agree to jointly push for ceasefire in Iran war
top.description: Wang Yi and Ishaq Dar aligned on an immediate ceasefire as Pakistan relays indirect talks between Washington and Tehran over a 15-point U.S. proposal.

top.title: Iran strikes Israel, Gulf states as IRGC warns civilians to flee US bases
top.description: On the 28th day of the war, Iran launched missiles and drones at Israel, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, and the UAE as Israel vowed to escalate strikes on Tehran.

top.title: Trump signals to allies no imminent Iran ground invasion
top.description: The reassurance comes as the Pentagon weighs sending 10,000 more troops and Sen. Murkowski drafts a war authorization bill.

top.title: Witkoff says he expects Iran talks 'this week' on 15-point peace plan
top.description: The U.S. envoy said Friday that Washington is awaiting Tehran's response to its proposal, delivered through Pakistan as intermediary.

top.title: Gas prices near $4 as states split on tax holidays
top.description: The national average hit $3.98, up a dollar in one month, spurring federal and state gas tax suspension proposals with mixed results.

top.title: Hegseth struck Black and female officers from Army promotion list
top.description: The defense secretary overrode Army leaders' objections, removing two Black and two female officers from a one-star general list, according to the New York Times.

top.title: Mexico says 40,000 of 130,000 missing may be alive
top.description: President Sheinbaum presented a long-awaited report on Mexico's disappearance crisis, revealing a national database plagued by gaps, duplicates, and missing data.

top.title: Iran-linked hackers breach FBI director's personal email
top.description: The Handala Hack Team posted photos and documents from Kash Patel's Gmail account; the DOJ confirmed the materials appear authentic.

top.title: Idaho passes strictest transgender bathroom ban with felony penalties
top.description: The bill, which now heads to the governor's desk, criminalizes restroom use in both government buildings and private businesses.

top.title: G7 agrees to multinational Hormuz escort mission after Iran war ends
top.description: Foreign ministers meeting in France committed to restoring freedom of navigation in the strait, with over 30 nations signaling readiness to contribute.

top.title: DOJ issues subpoenas in Paramount-Warner Bros merger probe
top.description: Federal investigators are examining the $110 billion deal's impact on streaming competition, content rights, and movie theaters.

top.title: RFK Jr. ally says White House now sees vaccines as 'losing issue'
top.description: A federal court ruling, a key adviser's resignation, and growing Senate resistance are forcing the administration to retreat from Kennedy's vaccine overhaul.

top.title: Rubio says US expects Iran war to end in weeks
top.description: The secretary of state told G7 ministers that U.S. objectives can be met without ground troops, calling the campaign ahead of schedule.

top.title: Controllers say LaGuardia needed more staff night of deadly crash
top.description: Six air traffic controllers told Reuters the airport's workload on March 22 warranted additional staffing before the fatal collision.

top.title: House ethics panel finds Rep. Cherfilus-McCormick guilty of 25 violations
top.description: A bipartisan committee ruled the Florida Democrat funneled $5 million in COVID-19 relief funds into her 2022 campaign, setting up a possible expulsion vote.

top.title: US land mines found near Shiraz kill civilians in first such deployment in decades
top.description: Weapons experts identified American-made BLU-91/B anti-tank mines scattered near an Iranian missile base, drawing condemnation from human rights groups.

top.title: Deployed nuclear warheads rose again in 2025, report finds
top.description: The annual Nuclear Weapons Ban Monitor counted 4,012 warheads on missiles, submarines, and bomber bases, up 108 from 2024.

top.title: Iran accuses US-Israel of genocide at UN Human Rights Council
top.description: Foreign Minister Araghchi addressed an emergency session Friday, calling the Minab school airstrike the "tip of a far larger iceberg" of war crimes.

top.title: US can confirm only a third of Iran's missiles destroyed
top.description: Intelligence assessments contradict Trump's claim that Iran has "very few rockets left" as the war nears its one-month mark.

top.title: SpaceX IPO sparks $15M in ticker bets before filing
top.description: Polymarket wagers on SpaceX's ticker symbol, valuation, and exchange have topped $15.2 million even though the company has yet to file IPO paperwork.

top.title: Buy America law stalls affordable housing amid HUD waiver backlog
top.description: Developers say HUD is taking six months or more to process waivers, adding hundreds of thousands of dollars in costs to federally funded projects.

top.title: IRGC urges civilians to flee areas near US forces as Trump extends Hormuz deadline
top.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard declared the Strait of Hormuz closed and threatened US military positions as Trump pushed his strike deadline to April 6.

top.title: Asia-Pacific governments rush to calm markets as Middle East war drives energy crisis
top.description: From emergency budgets to fuel rationing plans, countries across the region are deploying fiscal packages and liquidity measures to counter the economic fallout.

top.title: Iran halts two Chinese ships near Hormuz in rare reversal
top.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard hardened its blockade Friday, intercepting vessels and declaring the strait closed to ships linked to U.S. allies.

top.title: US confirms killing of IRGC Navy chief Tangsiri in Bandar Abbas strike
top.description: CENTCOM verified the death of the architect of Iran's Strait of Hormuz blockade, calling on remaining IRGC Navy members to abandon their posts.

top.title: Kremlin denies Putin asked oligarchs to fund Ukraine war costs
top.description: Spokesman Peskov said a billionaire volunteered a large donation on his own, contradicting reports that Putin directly appealed for budget contributions.

top.title: Study finds cells, firms and cities all slow in adding new functions as they grow
top.description: Researchers identified a shared mathematical pattern governing how complex systems diversify, from bacteria to corporations to government agencies.

top.title: US burns through 850-plus Tomahawks in Iran war, alarming Pentagon
top.description: More than 20% of the estimated U.S. Tomahawk stockpile has been expended in four weeks of strikes, the Washington Post reported.

top.title: Rubio calls Ukraine 'not America's war' as peace talks stall
top.description: U.S. Secretary of State Rubio's pointed remark at the G7 came as Russia launched a record 948-drone barrage and no new peace talks are scheduled.

top.title: ECB survey shows Iran war upended eurozone inflation outlook
top.description: Consumer confidence across Europe hit its lowest since October 2023 as the Middle East conflict drives energy prices higher and revives inflation fears.

top.title: Epstein survivors sue Trump admin and Google over exposed identities
top.description: About 100 victims filed a class action alleging the DOJ's mass document release exposed their identities and Google refused to remove the information.

top.title: Ukraine, Saudi Arabia sign defense deal during Zelensky's surprise visit
top.description: Kyiv is trading its battle-tested drone warfare expertise for advanced weapons and investment as both nations face Iranian-made threats.

top.title: China launches two trade barrier probes against U.S.
top.description: Beijing's Commerce Ministry opened investigations into U.S. supply chain disruptions and green trade restrictions, retaliating against Washington's Section 301 probes.

top.title: US has lost at least 16 aircraft in Iran war as losses mount
top.description: The toll includes an F-35A damaged by Iranian ground fire on March 19 — the first time the stealth jet has been struck by enemy fire.

top.title: Mediators say Iran never requested strike pause Trump cited
top.description: President Trump extended the deadline for energy strikes to April 6, but mediators told the WSJ that Tehran made no such request.

top.title: Anthropic discusses Q4 2026 IPO that could raise over $60B
top.description: Bankers competing for the mandate expect the AI startup's listing to rank among the largest IPOs in history, according to The Information.

top.title: Iran claims 1 million fighters ready for US ground invasion
top.description: Tehran says volunteers are flooding Basij and IRGC centers as the U.S. sends 82nd Airborne paratroopers and Marines to the region.

top.title: Experts warn Iran war's environmental toll could persist for decades
top.description: Over 400 environmentally harmful incidents have been logged since the conflict began, with toxic pollution threatening air, water, and soil across the Gulf.

top.title: UK pledges £100M more for Ukraine's air defenses
top.description: The additional funding brings Britain's air defense contributions to roughly £600 million over two months as Russia's aerial attacks persist.

top.title: Trump pushes back on report he wants speedy Iran deal
top.description: At a Thursday cabinet meeting, the president called The Wall Street Journal "terrible" and said Iran, not the U.S., is the side seeking a resolution.

top.title: Houthi leader signals readiness to join Iran war against US and Israel
top.description: Ansarallah has refrained from entering the conflict since it began Feb. 28 but controls the strategic Bab al-Mandeb Strait.

top.title: Iran offers Spain Hormuz access in first such EU overture
top.description: Tehran cited Madrid's "commitment to international law" after Spain barred US forces from using its bases for strikes on Iran.

top.title: Pentagon confirms first use of drone boats in Iran conflict
top.description: Uncrewed speedboats built by BlackSea Technologies have been patrolling in support of Operation Epic Fury, the Pentagon said Thursday.

top.title: Education Dept. to vacate headquarters for smaller office amid dismantling push
top.description: The move, planned for August, comes as the agency's building sits 70% vacant after more than a year of mass layoffs under the Trump administration.

top.title: Bipartisan Senate bill targets insider trading on prediction markets
top.description: Four senators introduced legislation Thursday to ban government officials from using nonpublic information to trade on prediction market platforms.

top.title: US senators introduce bill to sanction Hungary over Ukraine aid blockade
top.description: Sens. Shaheen and Tillis unveiled the bipartisan Block Putin Act targeting Hungarian officials as Orban continues to hold up a €90 billion EU loan to Kyiv.

top.title: Trump's signature to appear on US currency, ending 165-year tradition
top.description: The Treasury Department said $100 bills with the president's name will begin printing in June, replacing the Treasurer's signature for the first time since 1861.

top.title: Study finds AI chatbots affirm users 49% more than humans do
top.description: Stanford researchers found all 11 major AI systems tested exhibited sycophancy, giving overly agreeable advice that made users less willing to fix relationships.

top.title: Senate Democrats block photo ID voting amendment on party-line vote
top.description: All 47 Senate Democrats voted against a standalone photo ID requirement for federal elections, handing Republicans a midterm messaging win.

top.title: Deputy AG Blanche says DOJ purged all staff tied to Trump prosecutions
top.description: Speaking at CPAC on Thursday, Blanche said more than 200 people have left and no FBI agents involved in Trump cases remain.

top.title: US conducts hypersonic missile test at Cape Canaveral
top.description: Witnesses spotted an unscheduled launch Thursday from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station, identified by observers as a hypersonic missile test during a pre-announced window.

top.title: UK authorizes military to board Russian shadow fleet tankers
top.description: Starmer announced the move at a Helsinki summit as a drone struck a sanctioned oil tanker laden with Russian crude near the Bosporus.

top.title: US jobless claims rise to 210,000; continuing claims hit lowest since May 2024
top.description: Continuing claims fell to 1.819 million, suggesting laid-off workers are finding new jobs quickly despite broader economic uncertainty.

top.title: DOJ opens civil rights probes into three medical schools' admissions
top.description: Stanford, Ohio State, and UC San Diego must turn over seven years of applicant data by April 24 or risk losing federal funding.

top.title: Trump pushes aides to end Iran war within weeks
top.description: The president privately told advisers the conflict is in its final stages, urging them to stick to a four-to-six-week timeline.

top.title: OECD warns US inflation could hit 4.2% amid Iran war oil shock
top.description: The organization sharply raised its forecast from 3%, citing surging energy prices from the Middle East conflict that have disrupted global oil supplies.

top.title: Judge refuses to dismiss drug charges against Maduro
top.description: The former Venezuelan president appeared in a Manhattan federal court Thursday as his defense sought to toss the case over a legal fees dispute.

top.title: IBM says quantum computer matched lab data in materials simulation
top.description: Researchers say quantum simulations of a magnetic crystal reproduced neutron scattering experiments, a feat many believed was beyond current hardware.

top.title: Researchers recover 95% of battery metals using plasma and citric acid
top.description: The patented method uses a 15-minute microwave plasma pretreatment and lemon-strength acid to extract valuable minerals from spent lithium-ion batteries.

top.title: DOJ sues NewYork-Presbyterian over contracts that block cheaper care
top.description: The Justice Department alleges the hospital system's deals with insurers prevented New Yorkers from accessing lower-cost health plans.

top.title: Artemis 2 crew flies to Florida ahead of April 1 Moon launch
top.description: The four astronauts are set to arrive at Kennedy Space Center on Friday as final pad preparations continue for the first crewed lunar mission since Apollo.

top.title: Iran-backed FPV drones hit US helicopter, radar at Baghdad base
top.description: Footage shows strikes on a Black Hawk and a NASAMS-linked radar at Victory Base, escalating cheap-drone threats to American forces in Iraq.

top.title: Census: U.S. metro growth slowed sharply as immigration declined
top.description: Border communities saw the steepest drops, with Laredo's growth rate falling from 3.2% to 0.2% as net international migration was cut in half.

top.title: Iran demands Lebanon be included in any ceasefire deal
top.description: Tehran told mediators it will not accept a deal with the U.S. and Israel that does not halt the Israeli military campaign against Hezbollah.

top.title: LaGuardia wreckage towed off runway four days after fatal Air Canada crash
top.description: Most of the roughly 40 hospitalized passengers have been released, while the NTSB investigates overlapping failures including a missing transponder on the fire truck.

top.title: US kills four in 47th Caribbean boat strike since September
top.description: Operation Southern Spear's death toll has reached roughly 163 as rights groups and victims' families challenge the legality of the campaign.

top.title: France rules out military action to reopen Strait of Hormuz
top.description: Prime Minister Lecornu told the National Assembly that France "will not be drawn into a war that it did not choose" but will help secure shipping routes.

top.title: Iran’s expanding long-range missiles pose growing threat
top.description: Despite a 90% drop in launch rates, remaining Iranian missiles fired from hidden eastern bases are causing greater damage, according to analysts.

top.title: Aid groups plead for funds as Middle East war strains humanitarian system
top.description: UNHCR says operations are "dramatically underfunded" as millions are displaced and the dissolution of USAID leaves organizations scrambling to respond.

top.title: White House misses deadline to nominate new CDC director
top.description: The agency has lacked a permanent leader for over seven months as the administration struggles to find a candidate who can win Senate confirmation.

top.title: GOP lawmakers blast Pentagon over Iran war plans after briefing
top.description: House Armed Services Chair Mike Rogers warned of "consequences" after defense officials failed to explain troop deployments to lawmakers.

top.title: DOJ admits ICE arrested immigrants at courts under wrong legal authority
top.description: U.S. Attorney Jay Clayton said the government's defense of immigration courthouse arrests was based on a memo that only applied to criminal courthouses.

top.title: Senate Republicans probe abortion pill makers, press FDA on online sales
top.description: Sen. Cassidy and four GOP colleagues demanded compliance records from all three mifepristone manufacturers and urged the FDA to crack down on unauthorized sellers.

top.title: Iran claims missile strike on USS Abraham Lincoln as Gulf war widens
top.description: The IRGC launched attacks on Israel and U.S. bases in Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain as drones set fire to a fuel tank at Kuwait's airport.

top.title: 8th Circuit backs Trump's no-bond immigration detention policy
top.description: The 2-1 ruling is the second appellate victory for the administration's mass detention approach, despite opposition from hundreds of lower-court judges.

top.title: US offers $3M bounty for financial information on Haitian gangs
top.description: The reward targets financial networks of Viv Ansanm and Gran Grif, shifting tactics from previous bounties focused on individual gang leaders.

top.title: Six Arab states demand Iraq halt militia attacks from its territory
top.description: Baghdad summoned the U.S. envoy and vowed a UN complaint after a deadly airstrike on the Habbaniyah base killed seven soldiers.

top.title: Surgeon general pick stalls as GOP senators balk
top.description: Dr. Casey Means' nomination has languished for nearly 300 days, with key Republican senators still withholding support over vaccine views and qualifications.

top.title: JetBlue shares surge on report it hired advisers to explore sale
top.description: The struggling airline is reportedly evaluating potential deals with United Airlines, Alaska Airlines, or Southwest Airlines.

top.title: Iranian analyst warns Tehran ready to seize UAE, Bahrain coastlines
top.description: Morteza Simiari said on state TV that Iran's military has rehearsed coastal operations and will act if the U.S. "makes any mistake."

top.title: Pew poll finds 61% of Americans disapprove of Trump's Iran war
top.description: The survey of 3,524 adults, conducted March 16-22, also found 59% believe the U.S. made the wrong decision to use military force against Tehran.

top.title: Senate GOP launches reconciliation bill for Iran war, ICE and voter ID
top.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham said his Budget Committee will "expeditiously" draft a second party-line bill to bypass the filibuster, but key Republicans are skeptical.

top.title: Think tank proposes $100K cap on Social Security benefits
top.description: The Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget's "Six Figure Limit" plan would trim payouts to wealthy retirees as the program faces insolvency by 2032.

top.title: White House rejects Musk's offer to pay TSA workers
top.description: The rejection came as the DHS shutdown hit day 40, with a TSA official telling Congress that wait times have reached historic highs.

top.title: Pentagon signs deals to quadruple missile output amid Iran war
top.description: Lockheed Martin, BAE Systems, and Honeywell Aerospace agreed to accelerate production of key weapons as the U.S. replenishes wartime stockpiles.

top.title: Fed's operating losses fell to $19B in 2025 from $78B
top.description: The central bank's audited financial statements show interest expenses dropped sharply as rate cuts reduced what the Fed pays on bank reserves.

top.title: Raskin says DOJ memo shows Trump showed classified map on plane
top.description: A top House Democrat says the Trump administration's own Justice Department inadvertently handed over "damning evidence" from the classified documents probe.

top.title: EPA waives summer smog rules on gasoline amid Iran war price surge
top.description: The emergency waiver allows E15 ethanol-blend fuel sales starting May 1, as the Strait of Hormuz closure drives gas prices sharply higher.

top.title: Meta, YouTube found negligent in social media addiction trial
top.description: A Los Angeles jury awarded $3 million in damages to a young woman who alleged the platforms' addictive design features caused her mental health struggles.

top.title: White House says congressional approval not needed for Iran strikes
top.description: Spokeswoman Karoline Leavitt said the U.S. postponed strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure after "productive conversations" but warned Trump is "prepared to unleash hell."

top.title: Indiana bars Piece by Piece Autism Centers from Medicaid
top.description: The provider collected $29 million in 2023 to treat just 84 patients by exploiting a billing loophole that allowed reimbursements of up to $640 per hour.

top.title: Supreme Court rules ISPs not liable for users' piracy
top.description: The unanimous decision reverses a jury verdict against Cox Communications in a lawsuit led by Sony Music over illegal downloads of more than 10,000 songs.

top.title: NASA climate scientist Kate Marvel quits, citing Trump's 'attacks on science'
top.description: Marvel said a year of funding uncertainty and dismantled programs left her unable to do meaningful work at the Goddard Institute for Space Studies.

top.title: California Democrats' own poll shows two Republicans leading governor's race
top.description: The party warns its crowded field of eight candidates risks being shut out of the November general election under the state's top-two primary system.

top.title: Astronomers spot two planets forming around a young star like our sun
top.description: WISPIT 2 is only the second known system where two planets have been directly observed taking shape, offering a rare window into our solar system's origins.

top.title: Trump sets May 14-15 for rescheduled Beijing summit with Xi
top.description: The White House announced firm dates for the postponed China trip, which was delayed by the ongoing U.S. and Israeli war against Iran.

top.title: New York Times accuses Pentagon of defying court order on press access
top.description: The paper filed a new motion Tuesday after the department closed its press corridor and imposed escort requirements days after a judge struck down its media policy.

top.title: Prosecutor admitted no crime evidence in DOJ probe of Fed's Powell
top.description: A newly unsealed transcript shows the lead prosecutor told a judge his office had no evidence of criminal wrongdoing by the Fed chair.

top.title: TSA chief tells Congress agency won't be ready for World Cup
top.description: Nearly 460 officers have quit and unpaid payroll nears $1 billion as the DHS shutdown enters its 39th day, the acting TSA administrator testified.

top.title: US Army raises max enlistment age to 42, eases marijuana rules
top.description: Updated regulation published March 20 also drops the waiver requirement for a single marijuana possession conviction to speed up recruiting.

top.title: Israeli airstrike hits Christian town north of Beirut for first time
top.description: The strike on Sahel Alma marks a widening of Israel's air campaign into previously untouched areas of Lebanon amid the escalating war with Hezbollah.

top.title: Trump gets daily strike montage on Iran war, raising concerns
top.description: Officials told NBC News the curated two-minute videos emphasize U.S. successes while offering little detail on Iranian attacks or setbacks.

top.title: US assessing how to resume nuclear weapons testing
top.description: Undersecretary DiNanno told lawmakers the administration has made no decisions on a testing program but did not rule out underground explosive tests.

top.title: DHS shutdown delayed NTSB investigators probing deadly LaGuardia crash
top.description: NTSB Chair Jennifer Homendy said investigators waited hours in TSA lines, preventing the team from completing a full first day of work at the crash site.

top.title: US ships 30,000 barrels of fuel to Cuba's private sector
top.description: The Trump administration's dual strategy of blockading Cuba's government while supplying private businesses is gaining traction, according to Reuters.

top.title: US airstrikes kill 15 Iraq PMF fighters, including Anbar commander
top.description: The deadliest single strike since the US-Israeli war on Iran began also targeted PMF leader Falih al-Fayadh's residence in a separate attack.

top.title: Netanyahu taps top aide Dermer to vet US-Iran deal
top.description: Ron Dermer's mission focuses on ensuring Iran's nuclear program is dismantled and its enriched uranium stockpile eliminated, according to Bloomberg.

top.title: Democrat flips Florida House seat in Trump's Mar-a-Lago district
top.description: Emily Gregory defeated Trump-endorsed Republican Jon Maples in a district the president carried by about 10 points in 2024.

top.title: Anduril, Palantir building Golden Dome missile shield software
top.description: The Wall Street Journal reported the two firms are part of a nine-company consortium developing the $185 billion antimissile system's command-and-control layer.

top.title: Denmark's left bloc wins election but falls short of majority
top.description: Centrist leader Lars Løkke Rasmussen emerges as kingmaker with 14 seats as weeks of coalition talks loom.

top.title: Senate blocks third Democratic bid to curb Trump's Iran war powers
top.description: The 47-53 vote marked the third time this month Republicans defeated a resolution demanding congressional authorization for the military campaign.

top.title: Death toll rises to 69 in Colombia military plane crash
top.description: A C-130 Hercules went down shortly after takeoff Monday in the Amazon jungle, marking one of Colombia's deadliest military air disasters.

top.title: Iran says drones struck Israeli defense sites, Ben Gurion Airport
top.description: Tuesday's claimed strikes targeted Rafael Advanced Defense Systems and Israel Aerospace Industries as Operation True Promise 4 enters its fourth week.

top.title: Judge orders Trump to unfreeze $3B in Chicago transit funds
top.description: A federal judge granted a temporary restraining order releasing frozen rail funding, calling the administration's selective enforcement "pretextual."

top.title: Macron urges Iran to negotiate in third call with Pezeshkian since war began
top.description: The French president demanded an end to "unacceptable" attacks and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz as U.S. peace efforts intensify.

top.title: Debris from intercepted missiles knocks out 7 Kuwait power lines
top.description: Kuwait's Defense Ministry reported intercepting 17 ballistic missiles and 13 drones in 24 hours as the country endures its worst sustained bombardment since the Iran war began.

top.title: Iran stops all gas exports to Turkey after South Pars strike
top.description: The halt follows Israel's March 18 attack on the world's largest natural gas field, cutting off about 14% of Turkey's supply.

top.title: Lebanon expels Iran's ambassador as Israel vows to occupy southern Lebanon
top.description: Beirut declared Tehran's envoy persona non grata on Tuesday as Israel announced plans to seize territory up to the Litani River.

top.title: British troops down 14 drones in record night at Iraq base
top.description: RAF Regiment forces intercepted the largest single-night drone swarm since the Iran conflict began, as Tehran resumed attacks after an Eid lull.

top.title: Israel says it killed Quds Force operative in Beirut strike
top.description: The IDF and Shin Bet said a naval strike on Monday killed Muhammad Ali Kourani, who they described as an Iranian-directed operative planning attacks from Lebanon.

top.title: Saudi crown prince urges Trump to press for Iran regime's fall
top.description: Mohammed bin Salman has called the US-Israeli war a "historic chance" to reshape the Middle East as Gulf states edge toward open confrontation with Tehran.

top.title: Rubio testifies at ex-roommate's secret Venezuela lobbying trial
top.description: The secretary of state became the first sitting Cabinet member to take the stand in a criminal trial since 1983.

top.title: Moldova declares 60-day energy emergency after Russian strikes sever key power line
top.description: Parliament voted 72-0 to impose the emergency after overnight drone strikes on Ukraine knocked out the line supplying up to 70% of Moldova's electricity.

top.title: Hezbollah's missile stockpile down to one-sixth of pre-war levels, Israel says
top.description: Israel's military estimates the Iranian-backed group retains between 11,000 and 13,000 missiles and rockets, down from roughly 75,000 before October 2023.

top.title: NASA outlines moon strategy as Artemis II targets April launch
top.description: The agency is hosting a Washington event to detail how it plans to return astronauts to the lunar surface by 2028 despite ongoing delays.

top.title: Aid flotilla docks in Havana as US oil blockade deepens Cuba's crisis
top.description: The first vessel of the "Our America Convoy" delivered medical supplies, food, and solar panels, with two more ships expected this week.

top.title: Brazil court places Bolsonaro under house arrest on health grounds
top.description: Justice Alexandre de Moraes reversed months of denials, granting a 90-day house arrest after the ex-president was hospitalized with severe pneumonia.

top.title: Iran war drives fertilizer costs higher as U.S. planting season nears
top.description: Surging nitrogen prices, supplier shortages, and Strait of Hormuz disruptions are compounding years of losses for American grain farmers.
